23.anchor: Neither I nor my brothers nor my men nor the guards with me took off our clothes; each had his weapon, even when he went for water.
23.note: The meaning of the Hebrew for this clause is uncertain.

15.anchor: But the earlier governors—those preceding me—placed a heavy burden on the people and took forty shekels
15.note: That is, about 1 pound or about 460 grams

16.anchor: Instead, I devoted myself to the work on this wall. All my men were assembled there for the work; we
16.note: Most Hebrew manuscripts; some Hebrew manuscripts, Septuagint, Vulgate and Syriac I

70.anchor: Some of the heads of the families contributed to the work. The governor gave to the treasury 1,000 darics
70.note: That is, about 19 pounds or about 8.4 kilograms

71.anchor: Some of the heads of the families gave to the treasury for the work 20,000 darics
71.note: That is, about 375 pounds or about 170 kilograms; also in verse 72
That is, about 1 1/3 tons or about 1.2 metric tons

72.anchor: The total given by the rest of the people was 20,000 darics of gold, 2,000 minas
72.note: That is, about 1 1/4 tons or about 1.1 metric tons

5.anchor: The next section was repaired by the men of Tekoa, but their nobles would not put their shoulders to the work under their supervisors.

13.anchor: The Valley Gate was repaired by Hanun and the residents of Zanoah. They rebuilt it and put its doors with their bolts and bars in place. They also repaired a thousand cubits
13.note: That is, about 1,500 feet or about 450 meters

15.anchor: The Fountain Gate was repaired by Shallun son of Kol-Hozeh, ruler of the district of Mizpah. He rebuilt it, roofing it over and putting its doors and bolts and bars in place. He also repaired the wall of the Pool of Siloam,

16.anchor: Beyond him, Nehemiah son of Azbuk, ruler of a half-district of Beth Zur, made repairs up to a point opposite the tombs
16.note: Hebrew; Septuagint, some Vulgate manuscripts and Syriac tomb

18.anchor: Next to him, the repairs were made by their fellow Levites under Binnui
18.note: Two Hebrew manuscripts and Syriac (see also Septuagint and verse 24); most Hebrew manuscripts Bavvai
